rulings.Case Title: ERNEST MORGAN VS. MIRIAM Y. DRUHET-JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Ernest Morgan, Administrators Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday March 14, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF ERNEST MORGAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Ernest Morgan, Administrators Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence despite being granted an extension and an opportunity to file two oppositions. Defendant's Objections to Plaintiff's Evidence are overruled, except for objection number 11, which is sustained. Defendant's objection to the Court's order granting Defendant's request for continuance is unwarranted. Defendant was provided a chance to (1) fully comply with CCP 434c(h) by specifying what facts essential to justify opposition may exists to address specific material facts in the separate statement; (2) file timely opposition to the motion addressing all other bases for opposing the motion, e.g. objections, failure to shift burden, legal issues, etc.; (3) after filing of the initial opposition, concentrate on obtaining narrowly targeted discovery referenced above from November 30, 2018 (original due date of opposition) to February 28, 2019 (due date for supplemental opposition); (4) file a supplemental opposition addressing all remaining factual issues not referenced in the original opposition 3 months after the original due date. Plaintiff was given one opportunity to reply to both oppositions. Defendants' February 26, 2019 declaration does not provide any information regarding Defendants' attempts to obtain the "essential facts" (the purpose of continuance) from November 21, 2018 to January 29, 2019. On January 29, 2019 Defendants propounded requests for production by mail, with responses not due under the Discovery Act until after the due date for the supplemental opposition to the Motion for Summary Judgment. No supplemental opposition was filed. (The Court Notes that a Proof of Service was filed by Defendants on February 26, 2019 regarding service of documents on February 22, 2019 and February 26, 2019. It is unclear if any additional opposition papers were served at that time (no additional papers other than papers referenced above are on file) or this is service of opposition papers filed on November 30, 2018 and service of February 26, 2019 Declaration. The Court further notes that the server, Sherrell Johnson, (1) on February 22, 2019 claims to be (a) a resident of Hanckock County; (b) with mailing address in Bay St. Louis, MS; (c) familiar with firm's mailing practices in Los Angeles; and (d) executing the proof of service in Bay St. Louis, MS; and (2) on February 26, 2019 claims to be (a) a resident of Los Angeles County; (b) with mailing address in Bay St. Lois, MS; (c) familiar with firm's mailing practices in Los Angeles; and (d) executing the proof of service in Bay St. Louis, MS ). =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CSV HOSPITALITY MANAGEMENT LLC VS. SHAZAD SAGHAFI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment For Possession Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday March 14, 2019 line 6. PLAINTIFF CSV HOSPITALITY MANAGEMENT LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment For Possession Pursuant To Stipulation OFF CALENDAR, moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B. No courtesy copies of the moving papers provided to the Court. Additionally, the Court is in receipt of an unauthorized supplemental filing of March 13, 2019 addressing evidence of incidents purportedly pre-dating the filing of the motion, but not included in the moving papers. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE VS. MARGARET MARY FINN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday March 14, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT MARGARET FINN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Allegations in the complaint are sufficient to withstand a demurrer. Whether Plaintiff is a "government unit, agency, or authority" within the meaning of SFRO 37.2(r)(4) is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. =(501/REQ)
